pg22-p1-s3:

pg22-p1:
morning he searches madly for the bottle he misplaced the night before. If he can afford it, he may have liquor
concealed all over his house to be certain no one gets his entire supply away from him to throw down the wastepipe. As
matters grow worse, he begins to use a combination of high-powered sedative and liquor to quiet his nerves so he can go
to work. Then comes the day when he simply cannot make it and gets drunk all over again. Perhaps he goes to a doctor who
gives him morphine or some sedative with which to taper off. Then he begins to appear at hospitals and sanitariums.
pg22-p2-s1: This is by no means a comprehensive picture of the true alcoholic, as our behavior patterns vary.
pg22-p2-s2:
But this description
should identify him roughly.
pg22-p2:
This is by no means a comprehensive picture of the true alcoholic, as our behavior patterns vary. But this description
should identify him roughly.
pg22-p3-s1: Why does he behave like this?
pg22-p3-s2:
If hundreds of experiences have shown him that one drink means another debacle with all
its attendant suffering and humiliation, why is it he takes that one drink?
pg22-p3-s3: Why can't he stay on the water wagon?
pg22-p3-s4:
What
has become of the common sense and will power that he still sometimes displays with respect to other matters?
pg22-p3:
Why does he behave like this? If hundreds of experiences have shown him that one drink means another debacle with all
its attendant suffering and humiliation, why is it he takes that one drink? Why can't he stay on the water wagon? What
has become of the common sense and will power that he still sometimes displays with respect to other matters?
pg22-p4-s1: Perhaps there never will be a full answer to these questions.
pg22-p4-s2:
Opinions vary considerably as to why the alcoholic reacts
differently from normal people.
pg22-p4-s3: We are not sure why, once a certain point is reached, little can be done for him.
pg22-p4:
Perhaps there never will be a full answer to these questions. Opinions vary considerably as to why the alcoholic reacts
differently from normal people. We are not sure why, once a certain point is reached, little can be done for him. We
cannot answer the riddle.
pg22-p5-s1: We know that while the alcoholic keeps away from drink, as he may do for months or years, he reacts much like other men.
pg22-p5-s2:
We are equally positive that once he takes any alcohol whatever into his system, something happens, both in the bodily
and mental sense, which makes it virtually impossible for him to
